summary: State-operated long-term care facility for Alaska veterans.
eligibility: Alaska resident veterans.

benefit_name: Alaska Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Alaska offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Alaska veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Alaska is typically required. Contact the Alaska veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Alaska Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Alaska provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Alaska. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Alaska veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for permanently and totally disabled veterans.
eligibility: Veterans with 100% service-connected disability rating.

summary: Housing assistance and loan programs for eligible Alaska veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Alaska veterans.

summary: Free or discounted hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.
administering_agency: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

summary: Fee waivers for professional license applications for veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Business certification program for service-disabled veteran entrepreneurs.
eligibility: Veterans with service-connected disability rating of 10% or higher.

summary: Hiring preference in state government positions for eligible veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: State cemetery providing burial services for Alaska veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Education benefits for spouses and dependents of disabled or deceased veterans.
eligibility: Spouses and children of veterans with 100% disability or killed in service.

summary: Special veteran identification license plates for Alaska veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: In-state tuition waiver for Alaska resident veterans at state universities.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Alaska resident veterans.

summary: Veterans cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for Alabama veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and their eligible family members.

summary: Long-term care facility for Alabama veterans needing assisted living or skilled nursing care.

summary: CalVet operates eight state veterans homes providing skilled nursing, residential care, and adult day health services across California. Locations: Barstow, Chula Vista, Fresno, Lancaster, Redding, Ventura, West Los Angeles, and Yountville. Services include skilled nursing, assisted living, memory care, and outpatient day health programs.
eligibility: California resident veterans with honorable or general discharge. Income-based copayment scale applies for most services. VA-eligible veterans may use VA per diem payments to offset costs. Spouses of eligible veterans may also qualify for some facilities.

summary: Educational assistance for dependents of disabled veterans.
eligibility: Dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

eligibility: Veterans with 100% service-connected disability rating.

summary: Free hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.

summary: CalVet offers competitive-rate home loans to California veterans through a state-originated mortgage structure. CalVet purchases the home and holds title in a land contract arrangement, providing veterans with below-market interest rates and built-in insurance benefits. No private mortgage insurance (PMI) required.
eligibility: California resident veterans with honorable discharge. Must intend to occupy the home as primary residence. Farm, home, and mobile home loan options available.

summary: In-state tuition rates for California resident veterans at UC and CSU.
eligibility: Honorably discharged California veterans.

summary: Expedited recognition of military-earned professional licenses and certifications.
eligibility: Veterans with military training in licensed professions.

summary: Business certification and contracting preference for service-disabled veterans.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Hiring preference for state government positions for veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for California veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

benefit_name: California SBDC Veteran Business Program & CalVet Business Resources
summary: California connects veteran entrepreneurs with the SBDC (Small Business Development Center) Veteran Business Program, which provides free one-on-one business advising, training, and technical assistance at centers statewide. CalVet partners with the SBDC network and also maintains a business resources portal covering licensing fee waivers, DVBE certification, and state contracting opportunities for veteran-owned businesses.
eligibility: Honorably discharged California veterans interested in starting or growing a business. SBDC services are free of charge. Business advising, loan packaging, and training available at SBDC centers throughout California.
administering_agency: California SBDC Network / California Department of Veterans Affairs (CalVet)

summary: Distinctive veteran identification license plates for California veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

benefit_name: Florida Scholarships for Children and Spouses of Deceased/Disabled Veterans (CSDDV)
summary: The Florida CSDDV scholarship provides educational assistance to spouses and children of Florida veterans who died or were permanently and totally disabled from service-connected causes. Covers tuition, fees, and room/board at Florida public institutions for up to 110% of required credit hours. Awards are renewable annually.
eligibility: Spouses and dependent children of Florida veterans who died from service-connected disability, or who have a 100% permanent and total service-connected disability rating, or who died on active duty. Florida residency required. Apply through OSFA.
administering_agency: Florida Office of Student Financial Assistance (OSFA)

benefit_name: Florida Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Florida offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Florida veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Florida is typically required. Contact the Florida veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Florida Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Florida provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Florida. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Florida veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

benefit_name: Florida Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemptions
summary: Florida provides two property tax exemptions for disabled veterans: (1) a $5,000 exemption for veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating, applied against assessed value; (2) a full property tax exemption for veterans with a total and permanent service-connected disability, or who are rated 100% disabled and confined to a wheelchair due to service injury (F.S. §196.081). Surviving spouses may continue to receive the full exemption after the veteran's death.
eligibility: Florida resident veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability for the $5,000 exemption. Full exemption requires total and permanent service-connected disability or 100% rating with wheelchair confinement. Applied through the county property appraiser, not the DVA.
administering_agency: Florida County Property Appraisers (administered locally, not DVA)

summary: Florida operates six state veterans nursing homes offering skilled nursing and domiciliary care for eligible Florida veterans. Homes located in Baldomero Lopez (Land O' Lakes), Emilio Hernandez (Miami), Alexander Nininger (Pembroke Pines), Clifford Chester Sims (Springfield), Robert Jenkins (Lake City), and Clyde Wells (Daytona Beach). Priority admission for veterans with service-connected conditions.
eligibility: Florida resident veterans with honorable discharge. VA per diem payments may apply for eligible veterans. Sliding-scale copayments based on income. Contact FL DVA for current admission status and waitlist information.
administering_agency: Florida Department of Veterans' Affairs (FL DVA)

benefit_name: Florida Salute Our Soldiers & Hometown Heroes Housing Programs
summary: Florida offers two veteran-priority housing assistance programs: Salute Our Soldiers (SOS) Military Loan Program provides below-market first mortgages with down payment assistance for active duty and veterans; Florida Hometown Heroes provides up to $35,000 in down payment and closing cost assistance for income-eligible homebuyers including veterans.
eligibility: Florida resident veterans and active duty service members. Income limits apply for Hometown Heroes program. Must be first-time homebuyer or meet exceptions.

benefit_name: Florida Free Hunting and Fishing Licenses for Disabled Veterans
summary: Florida provides free multi-year hunting and fishing licenses to qualified disabled veterans. Veterans with a 100% service-connected disability receive a free 5-year comprehensive hunting and fishing license. Veterans with 50% or greater service-connected disability receive a free freshwater and saltwater fishing license. Licenses are issued through Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission (FWC).
eligibility: 100% service-connected disability: free 5-year comprehensive license. 50%+ service-connected disability: free fishing license. Florida resident veterans only. Documentation: VA benefit summary letter showing disability percentage.
administering_agency: Florida Fish and Wildlife Conservation Commission (FWC)

benefit_name: Florida CW Bill Young Tuition Waiver for Veterans
summary: The Congressman C.W. Bill Young Tuition Waiver Program waives out-of-state tuition at Florida public universities and colleges for honorably discharged veterans and eligible dependents using VA education benefits. Veterans pay in-state tuition rates regardless of state residency at the time of enrollment.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans using VA education benefits (Chapter 30, 31, 33, or 35) at a Florida public institution. Also covers eligible veterans pursuing a degree while serving on active duty. Apply through the veterans services office at the institution.
administering_agency: Florida Office of Student Financial Assistance (OSFA)

summary: Under Florida law (HB 7015 / §455.02 F.S.), the Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR) waives initial application and licensing fees for honorably discharged veterans and their spouses within 60 months of the veteran's discharge. Covers 20+ regulated professions including real estate, cosmetology, contracting, interior design, and others under DBPR jurisdiction.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Florida veterans within 60 months of discharge. Surviving spouses of veterans who died from service-connected disability also eligible. Applies to initial license applications only (not renewals). Eligible professions are those regulated by DBPR.
administering_agency: Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation (DBPR)

benefit_name: Florida Service-Disabled Veteran Business Enterprise Certification
summary: Florida's Office of Supplier Diversity (OSD) certifies Service-Disabled Veteran Business Enterprises (SDVBEs). Certified businesses receive preference in state procurement and a 5-point preference in competitive bidding. The state tracks spending with SDVBEs and agencies have annual utilization goals.
eligibility: Businesses at least 51% owned and controlled by one or more veterans with a service-connected disability. Owner must be actively involved in day-to-day operations. Apply through the FL Department of Management Services OSD portal.
administering_agency: Florida Department of Management Services — Office of Supplier Diversity (OSD)

summary: Florida law (§295, Florida Statutes) grants veterans and eligible family members preference in state government hiring, promotion, and retention. Point credits are added to examination scores: 10 points for veterans with service-connected disability, 5 points for other eligible veterans. Applies to all state agencies, universities, colleges, counties, and municipalities.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Florida veterans. Additional preference for veterans with service-connected disabilities, spouses of veterans with total and permanent service-connected disabilities, and surviving spouses of veterans who died in service. Must apply through employer's HR using DD-214 as documentation.

summary: Cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for Florida veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

summary: Florida offers a variety of specialty license plates recognizing veteran status, including branch-specific plates (Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines, Coast Guard), Combat Veteran, Disabled Veteran, Purple Heart, POW/MIA, and more. Florida waives the specialty plate annual use fee for veterans with 100% service-connected disability. Applications through county tax collector offices or FLHSMV.
eligibility: Honorably discharged Florida veterans. Fee waiver on specialty plates for 100% service-connected disabled veterans. Some plates require documentation of qualifying award or status.
administering_agency: Florida Department of Highway Safety and Motor Vehicles (FLHSMV)

summary: Educational assistance for spouses and dependents of disabled veterans in Georgia.
eligibility: Spouses and dependents of veterans with 100% service-connected disability.

benefit_name: Georgia Veteran Survivor and Dependent Benefits
summary: Georgia offers state-level benefits for surviving spouses, dependents, and family members of veterans. Programs may include education tuition waivers or scholarships, survivor pension supplements, priority access to state veteran services, and continued eligibility for select state programs. Specific programs and eligibility requirements vary.
eligibility: Surviving spouses, unremarried widows/widowers, and dependent children of eligible Georgia veterans. Some programs require the veteran to have had a service-connected disability or to have died in service. Residency in Georgia is typically required. Contact the Georgia veterans agency for current program availability.

benefit_name: Georgia Enhanced Benefits for Service-Connected Disabled Veterans
summary: Georgia provides a package of enhanced state benefits for veterans with a service-connected disability rating. Benefits may include increased property tax exemptions at higher rating tiers, priority access to state veteran services, healthcare facility preference, adaptive equipment assistance, and waived fees across multiple state programs. Specific benefits and thresholds vary by rating level.
eligibility: Veterans with any service-connected disability rating who are residents of Georgia. Many programs require a minimum 10% rating; higher-value benefits typically require 50% or 100% ratings. Contact the Georgia veterans agency for current eligibility thresholds.

summary: Property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities in Georgia.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: State-operated long-term care facility for eligible Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Georgia resident veterans with honorable discharge.

summary: Housing assistance and loan programs for eligible Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

summary: Discounted or free hunting and fishing licenses for disabled veterans in Georgia.
eligibility: Disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities.

summary: In-state tuition rates for veterans at Georgia public universities and colleges.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans who are state residents.

summary: Exemption or exclusion of military retirement income from Georgia state income tax.
eligibility: Veterans receiving military retirement pay.

summary: Fee waivers for professional license applications for Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Business certification and support for service-disabled veteran entrepreneurs in Georgia.
eligibility: Veterans with 10% or higher service-connected disability rating.

summary: Hiring preference for state government positions for Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.

summary: Cemetery providing burial services and perpetual care for Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans and eligible family members.

summary: Special veteran identification license plates for Georgia veterans.
eligibility: Honorably discharged veterans.
